provenance.note: First observation: a refine-phase run died mid-run, leaving an empty branch, but the spec-pending-review label was applied anyway
idle_timeout theory narrowed to refine-push/plan-push-and-advance depending only on the ephemeral runner exit code, not on whether a spec/plan artifact was actually committed

Root cause confirmed via kept-container transcript: the agent parks on ScheduleWakeup, gets killed, but the DAG node is still marked dag_node_completed, so downstream push+label nodes fire on an empty branch
Definitive: a command node closes the instant the agent ends its turn, even with a successfully scheduled wakeup pending — this finding is what CLAUDE.md's 'Turn end = process end' rule now encodes
docs: bring over approved spec/plan for issue #212
feat(workflow): add push_gate_check.sh — git-aware spec/plan artifact check
fix(workflow): gate refine-push/plan-push-and-advance on committed artifact existence (#212)

prefix.established_state: A refine or plan phase agent may end its turn (deliberately, via ScheduleWakeup, or by being killed) without having committed a spec/plan artifact for the issue.

suffix.outcome: The next DAG stage (refine-push / plan-push-and-advance) had no way to know whether the prior phase actually produced a committed artifact — it trusted the ephemeral run's completion status alone, so an agent's mid-phase death silently produced a stranded, mislabeled ticket (spec-pending-review or plan-pending-review with nothing to review) at least twice (issues #43 and #41 per the thread).
suffix.verifier_signal: Root-cause comment #212#issuecomment-4934672132; fixed by push_gate_check.sh (cebe413) wired into both nodes (a42b029), which checks the committed git diff rather than the ephemeral run status

annotation.notes: The richest and most explicitly self-documented case in either repo's history — a 4-comment root-cause evolution over ~7.5 hours, directly named in CLAUDE.md's current 'Turn end = process end' rule. Companion fixture policy-violated-before-side-effect-02 covers the same issue's gate-label angle under a distinct pivot/lens.
